title: hapalochilus novae-hiberniae
intro: Hapalochilus novae-hiberniae, also known as the New Hebrides orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, sore throats, and skin infections. Hapalochilus novae-hiberniae can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for various health benefits.
